name: Flüela
comment: Talus slope above and SW of lake Schottensee with 2 boreholes, 1 at base, 1 in upper sector of slope. Permafrost only at the base of the slope (avalanche debris in spring) and rock glacier at the Schwarzhorn (only TLS). IMIS (meteo) station NE of the lake. An automatic camera takes pictures of the talus slope every hour.

name: Gemmi
comment: Small valley with highly active rock glaciers (warm permafrost), solifluction lobes, and patterned grounds. Detailed site description in B. Krummenacher, D. Mihajlovic, A. Nussbaum, and B. Staub (Eds.), "20 Jahre Furggent<e4>lti. Permafrostuntersuchungen auf der Gemmi," Bern: Geographica Bernensia, 2008.

name: Gemsstock
comment: Rocky ridge with a NW and a SE facing rock wall (40-50 m high). Horizontal 40 m borehole through ridge. Meteostation at N foot of ridge (below borehole). Automatic cameras take pictures hourly on both sides. TLS on both sides.

name: Gentianes
comment: The site is located on the left lateral moraine of the Glacier de Tortin, near the Col des Gentianes.

name: Jungfrau
comment: Two boreholes in the Jungfrau East ridge (only the one on the North side is instrumented) and five rock-surface-temperature measurements in the area.

name: Les Attelas
comment: Talus slope in the west-facing flank of Mont Gel<e9> with three boreholes, GST measurements, and geophysical monitoring. Good site description in C. Scapozza, C. Lambiel, L. Baron, L. Marescot, and E. Reynard, "Internal structure and permafrost distribution in two alpine periglacial talus slopes, Valais, Swiss Alps," Geomorphology, vol. 132(3-4), pp. 208-221, 2011.

name: Matterhorn
comment: Borehole-temperature measurements in crest (Hoernligrat) above and close to Hoernlihuette.

name: Muot da Barba Peider
comment: NW oriented, 40<b0> steep talus slope equipped with avalanche defence structures and boreholes above Ursina rock glacier (Foura da l'amd Ursina).

name: Ritigraben
comment: Rock glacier above Ritigraben gully. Borehole and meteo station. TLS.

name: Schafberg
comment: Active rock glacier  (Foura da l'amd Ursina) above Pontresina. Two boreholes and TLS. Rock glacier tongue situated in Val Giandains gully above village.

name: Schilthorn
comment: Debris-covered E-W oriented crest. Most measurement installations in the N slope about 60 m below the summit, but some GST measurements also further down as well as in the S slope. Further information can be found e.g. in J. Noetzli, C. Hilbich, C. Hauck, M. Hoelzle, and S. Gruber (2008): Comparison of simulated 2D temperature profiles with time-lapse electrical resistivity data at the Schilthorn crest, Switzerland. In 9th International Conference on Permafrost. Fairbanks, US, 1293-1298.
